key_insight: Outlier-free divergence-conforming isogeometric discretizations preserve stable wave propagation in poroelastic media and make explicit timesteps nearly independent of approximation order.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient and robust explicit simulations of coupled solid-fluid wave phenomena with fewer degrees of freedom and larger stable timesteps than standard high-order finite elements.
method: Primal poroelastic formulation with matrix displacement, fluid displacement, and pressure.
Reduced formulation with pressure eliminated; both use divergence-conforming spline spaces for displacement fields.
Spectral analysis in 1D and timestep scaling studies in 2D, including fast/slow wave decomposition.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that spectra match analytical expectations and that normalized timestep scaling is demonstrated in 2D, showing order-independent behavior for outlier-free IGA.
watch_item: Benefits depend on conforming spline spaces and outlier removal; nonconforming discretizations can lose stability, correct null spaces, or the timestep advantage.
paper_title: Isogeometric Analysis for Explicit Wave Propagation in Poroelastic Media
